input.headline: William and Catherine meet Paddington at Royal Variety Performance

raw.headline: William and Catherine meet Paddington at Royal Variety Performance

winner: William and Catherine arrived late to the Royal Variety Performance, expecting a polite bear—only for Paddington to arrive early with a surprise invitation: "Would you like to meet *your* royal highnesses?

candidates.text: Why did Paddington refuse to shake hands with Prince William? Because he was *bear-ing* a grudge against royal *paws*.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative wordplay with 'bear-ing' and 'paws,' specific to the characters involved, concise and surprising."}

candidates.text: The royal couple was spotted stuffing Paddington with marmalade sandwiches until he started floating like a balloon—apparently, they forgot he’s already been around the world once by this point.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist on Paddington's backstory, concise and specific with a playful pun on 'floating like a balloon'."}

candidates.text: William and Catherine meet Paddington at the Royal Variety Performance — and suddenly, the Queen is asking for a stiff upper lip.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with the Queen, specific reference to Royal Variety Performance, concise and natural humor."}

candidates.text: After meeting the royal family, Paddington looked at the Queen and asked if he could finally have a marmalade sandwich, to which William replied, "I'm afraid you'll have to wait in line behind the other three bears in the palace.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'three bears' add surprise and specificity; ties well with the headline and characters involved."}

candidates.text: William and Catherine arrived late to the Royal Variety Performance, expecting a polite bear—only for Paddington to arrive early with a surprise invitation: "Would you like to meet *your* royal highnesses?
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with Paddington taking the initiative, specific reference to royal titles, concise and natural phrasing."}

candidates.text: The Royal Family really should be careful with the guest list, because meeting Paddington is a very high-stakes tea party.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Puns on 'high-stakes tea party' are clever and specific to the context, with a touch of surprise and natural humor."}

candidates.text: William and Catherine met Paddington at the Royal Variety Performance — it was a bear-ly believable royal encounter.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Creative pun on 'barely believable' with 'bear-ly', ties in the bear character from Paddington, and fits the royal context well."}

candidates.text: William and Catherine met Paddington at the Royal Variety Performance, where he insisted on giving a speech in Peruvian.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising twist with Paddington speaking Peruvian, specific reference to the event, concise and natural."}

candidates.text: The Royal Variety Performance was briefly delayed when the Queen had to be escorted away because Paddington refused to leave the stage until he was served a marmalade sandwich on a silver platter.
candidates.judge_raw: {"score": 8.5, "reason": "Surprising and specific twist with Paddington's demand, ties well to the headline, and uses natural English with a touch of whimsy."}
